rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use visible identifiers from the current case when available, otherwise rely on case-provided thresholds and legal feature names
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes for output dependency contracts
generic_rules: Validate AGREE annex placement against AADL scope rules to maintain architectural integrity
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names when explicit identifiers are unavailable
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatch diagnostics
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume` and `guarantee` for behavioral contracts, `eq` for equations, and `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit port or constant exposure
Do not place AGREE annex blocks in invalid scopes that would disrupt AADL architecture
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
